description: Synthetic topic seeds shaped like topicBank entries, used as the stand-in for xAI X Search results when the live path is disabled. No real X posts, no real handles, no real URLs. Stage 6.1.5.

topics.title: Pitch clock changed baseball pacing.
topics.resolution: The pitch clock made baseball faster and more watchable.
topics.thesisFraming: The pitch clock made baseball faster and more watchable. Late innings have less downtime and the pacing keeps fans in the game.
counterClaims: The pitch clock rushed pitchers into mistakes and hurt late-inning pacing.
Faster pacing in baseball cheapened the strategic pauses that matter.

evidenceFacts.label: MLB average game length change
evidenceFacts.sourceText: Average MLB game length dropped by roughly 24 minutes in the first season of the pitch clock.

evidenceFacts.label: Player and viewer reaction surveys
evidenceFacts.sourceText: Player and viewer reaction surveys showed mixed but largely positive responses to the pitch clock.

tangentHooks: Defense-first teams are more fun than highlight teams — separate thread.

topics.title: Bike lanes are better curb space than parking.
topics.resolution: Protected bike lanes are a better use of curb space than parking in dense corridors.
topics.thesisFraming: Protected bike lanes are a better use of curb space than parking in dense corridors. Bike lanes move more people per linear curb foot than parking does.
counterClaims: Protected bike lanes do not actually outperform parking for most curb users in most corridors.
Replacing curb parking with bike lanes hurts businesses that need curb loading.

evidenceFacts.label: Curb productivity studies
evidenceFacts.sourceText: Curb productivity research consistently finds protected bike lanes carry more daily users per foot than parking in dense corridors.

evidenceFacts.label: Business-impact case studies
evidenceFacts.sourceText: Multiple corridor studies show retail revenue stable or up after replacing curb parking with protected bike lanes.

tangentHooks: Libraries should host more after-hours events — separate thread.

topics.title: Long onboarding is an apology for bad UI.
topics.resolution: Long onboarding screens are usually an apology for bad UI.
topics.thesisFraming: Long onboarding screens are usually an apology for bad UI. The onboarding patches over the bad UI the product team did not fix at the core.
counterClaims: Long onboarding screens are not usually an apology — they are a deliberate guided first session.
Calling long onboarding an apology underestimates the value of structured first sessions.

evidenceFacts.label: Onboarding drop-off studies
evidenceFacts.sourceText: Onboarding drop-off studies consistently find longer flows correlate with higher early-funnel drop.

evidenceFacts.label: Activation metric analysis
evidenceFacts.sourceText: Activation metric analysis often shows users who skip long onboarding still activate at similar rates.

tangentHooks: A useful empty state beats a tutorial — separate thread.
